description_long: This object consists of six cylindrical red sticks bundled together with two dark straps, resembling classic dynamite sticks. A rectangular panel, likely an electronic timer or control unit, is mounted on the top center of the bundle. Multiple colored wires—red, yellow, green, and blue—are coiled and connected between the sticks and the control unit, suggesting a complex wiring system for detonation. The ends of the sticks are capped and show a rough, paper-like texture, typical of explosive cartridges. The overall construction is compact and utilitarian, with exposed wiring and a prominent control panel. The design is visually reminiscent of improvised explosive devices often depicted in media.
description: A bundle of six red dynamite sticks strapped together with a central control panel and multiple coiled colored wires. The object features exposed wiring and a prominent timer or control unit.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular control panel and the coiled colored wires attached to the top of the dynamite bundle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the circular ends of the six dynamite sticks and the dense arrangement of wires looping around the bundle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the dynamite bundle with visible wires and straps, but no control panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the other ends of the dynamite sticks and the colorful wires, with the control panel not visible.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation appears to be controlled via a central electronic panel, with multiple colored wires likely serving as triggers or fuses for the explosive sticks.
